mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue trajectories and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed 0.5% MoM growth
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during a high-inflation environment reflect supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints, which compress profit margins and force negative S&P 500 repricing over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during high inflation periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in >65% of instances, or the regime filter adds no predictive power over unconditional models.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate hike exceeding 0.50 percentage points in a single meeting signals a sharp monetary policy pivot that immediately compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Large rate hikes are followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate consumer spending capacity and supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth periods yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of instances, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real personal consumption expenditures indicates weakening consumer demand relative to broader output, which signals impending earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Divergence periods produce positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable employment during a post-recession recovery phase indicates expanding capacity utilization without wage pressure, supporting margin expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward testing.
